description: Discovery (keyword search and similarity search) MUST scope results by the requesting agent's effective audience. Restricted/private contexts MUST NOT appear in matches for unauthorized requesters AND MUST NOT contribute to total_estimate (the count itself leaks existence). Per RFC-ACDP-0005 §4 and RFC-ACDP-0008 §4.5.

preconditions.notes: Both contexts match the search query 'q=Q3 sales' on the title field. Visibility scoping determines what each requester sees.

scenarios.name: Authorized requester (in audience of restricted context)

expected.notes: Authorized requester sees both. Match order is registry-defined.

scenarios.name: Unauthorized requester (NOT in audience of restricted context)

expected.notes: MUST be exactly 1 — NOT 2. Including the restricted context in total_estimate (even without listing it in matches) leaks its existence to the unauthorized requester. Registry MUST scope BOTH the matches array AND the total_estimate count.
Anonymous requesters see only public contexts. Same scoping rule as authenticated unauthorized requesters: restricted/private contexts MUST NOT contribute to total_estimate.

scenarios.name: Anonymous requester (registry advertises anonymous_public_reads=false or unset)

expected.notes: Registry that does not advertise anonymous_public_reads MUST reject anonymous requests outright (RFC-ACDP-0008 §6.3).
